1.name: Task Browsing
1.summary: Signed-in users see a role-scoped table of tasks (Admins see all tasks with assignee; Users see only their own), with empty and error states; auth and role-based access gating are foundational to this epic.
nonGoals: No public sign-up or self-registration — accounts are created for you out of band.
No password reset, single sign-on, or multi-factor sign-in.
No dark mode — the app is light mode only.
No searching or sorting the task list in this epic — only the role-scoped view and filtering by status.
Users will not see tasks assigned to other team members.
infrastructureReuseNotes: API client lives at web/src/lib/api/client.ts — use its get/post/put/del helpers for the task-list fetch; never call fetch() directly (CLAUDE.md §2).
Shared API types live in web/src/types/api.ts — extend it with the Task and User shapes from brief §6 rather than introducing a parallel type module.
Validation schemas live in web/src/lib/validation/schemas.ts — add the sign-in credentials schema here (Zod) rather than inline.

The root layout web/src/app/layout.tsx already wraps the app in ToastProvider + ToastContainer — per CLAUDE.md §6, add SessionProvider by editing this existing layout, and reuse the toast context for transient error feedback.

Shadcn primitives already present: button, card, input, label in web/src/components/ui/ — compose the sign-in form and task table from these (CLAUDE.md §1); add a Shadcn table primitive via mcp__shadcn__add_component rather than hand-rolling.
No auth utilities exist yet — next-auth (credentials provider), the auth config, the SessionProvider wrapper, and the role-based access gate are all new and introduced by Story 1.
Brand palette is light-only: primary #2563EB, row/sidebar background #F3F4F6 — reference tokens in web/src/app/globals.css; no hex literals in components.

1.title: View the task list
1.plainSummary: After signing in, a User lands on the Tasks page and sees a table of the tasks assigned to them with title, due date, and status; an Admin instead sees every task across the team with an extra assignee column. Anyone not signed in is sent to the sign-in page first.
1.summary: Establishes the authentication + session foundation (next-auth credentials provider, SessionProvider in the root layout, a /sign-in page, redirect-to-sign-in for unauthenticated access, and role-based access gating) and delivers the role-scoped /tasks list as one routable vertical slice. Fetches tasks via the API client through the mock layer; Users see only tasks where they are the assignee, Admins see all tasks with an assignee column. Includes the loading state.

acceptanceCriteria.text: An unauthenticated visitor opening any app page is redirected to the sign-in page.
A User who signs in lands on the Tasks page and sees a table showing only their own tasks with title, due date, and status columns.
An Admin who signs in sees a table of all tasks across the team, including an assignee column.
A User cannot see tasks assigned to other team members in the list.
While tasks are being fetched, a loading indicator is shown before the table renders.
A signed-in User who navigates to an Admin-only route is redirected or shown a permission-denied state rather than the admin content.

manualTestChecklist: Open the app while signed out (or in a fresh private window) → you are taken to the sign-in page.
Sign in as a User → you land on the Tasks page showing only the tasks assigned to you, with title, due date, and status.
Confirm none of the rows show tasks belonging to other team members.
Sign in as an Admin → you see every task across the team, plus a column showing who each task is assigned to.
As a signed-in User, type an admin-only address into the URL bar → you see a 'you don't have permission' state on the page, not the admin content.
Reload the Tasks page → you briefly see a loading indicator before the table appears.
specGaps: Story 1 implies GET /v1/tasks returning the task list, filtered by assignee for the User role — the API spec does not yet exist (dataSource: api-in-development), so design-api-agent must document this list endpoint and its role-scoping.
Story 1 implies a sign-in credential check (email + password) — frontend-only auth has no documented endpoint; the credentials provider validates against mock/seed user data.

2.title: Empty and error states
2.plainSummary: A User who has no tasks assigned sees a friendly "No tasks assigned to you yet" message instead of an empty table. If the task list fails to load, everyone sees an error message with a Retry button that tries again.
2.summary: Adds the empty-state and load-error handling to the /tasks list from Story 1. When the task fetch returns no rows for the current user, render an explicit empty-state message. When the fetch fails, render an error message with a Retry affordance that re-issues the same request and recovers to the populated table on success.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A User with no assigned tasks sees the message "No tasks assigned to you yet" instead of an empty table.
When the task list fails to load, the user sees an error message describing the failure with a Retry button.
Clicking Retry after a failure re-requests the task list and renders the populated table when the request succeeds.

manualTestChecklist: Sign in as a User who has no tasks assigned → you see 'No tasks assigned to you yet' instead of an empty table.
With the task list set to fail loading → you see an error message and a Retry button.
Click Retry while the data is available again → the task table loads and the error message disappears.

2.name: Task Actions
2.summary: Admins create, edit, reassign, and delete tasks (with confirm-before-delete and due-date editing), and the assignee marks their own task complete; all write actions are gated to the permitted role.
nonGoals: No email notifications or due-date reminders when a task is created, assigned, or changed.
No task comments, attachments, or activity history.
No bulk actions — tasks are created, edited, and deleted one at a time.
Deleting a task removes it permanently — there is no trash or undo.
infrastructureReuseNotes: Typed endpoints already exist in web/src/lib/api/endpoints.ts — use createTask(), updateTask(), deleteTask(), getUsers(), getCurrentUser(); do NOT add new client wrappers or call fetch() directly (§2).

The /tasks/new route already exists as an admin-gated placeholder (web/src/app/tasks/new/page.tsx, wrapped in RequireRole role=admin + AppShell) — Story 1 replaces the placeholder body with the form, keeping the existing RequireRole gate.
Reuse web/src/components/auth/RequireRole.tsx for the Create entry point and Delete control visibility (BR1, R9); do not hand-roll role checks.

The Admin All tasks list is rendered by web/src/components/tasks/TasksView.tsx (fetch/loading/error/empty lifecycle, reloadKey-driven refetch) and TaskTable.tsx — Story 1's post-create refresh and Story 2's row removal hook into this existing state/refetch mechanism; extend, don't fork.

Error+Retry UX already has a working pattern in TasksView (role=alert region + Retry Button driving reloadKey) and a toast system at web/src/components/toast/ — reuse one rather than inventing a new error surface (R12, NFR5).
Zod validation lives in web/src/lib/validation/schemas.ts with validateRequest() and formFieldSchemas (incl. dateString) — add a createTask schema following the signInSchema pattern.
No Shadcn select or dialog primitive is installed yet — the assignee dropdown (Story 1) and confirm dialog (Story 2) require adding them via mcp__shadcn__add_component (§1); compose, don't hand-roll.

1.title: Create a task
1.plainSummary: An Admin opens the New Task form, fills in a title and picks an assignee (both required), optionally adds a description and a due date, and saves. The new task appears in the All tasks list right away without reloading the page. A User never sees a Create button or the New Task form.
1.summary: Build the Admin task-creation flow on the existing /tasks/new route. Form with title, description, due date, and an assignee dropdown populated from getUsers(); validate title/due date/assignee present before submit (BR5); submit via createTask() (R5, R8) and surface the new task in the All tasks list without a full reload. Create entry point on /tasks is admin-only (BR1). New tasks created with status pending (BR4). Errors show an inline/toast message with retry (R12/NFR5).

acceptanceCriteria.text: An Admin on /tasks/new sees the create form with title, description, due date, and assignee fields.
The assignee selector lists team members fetched from the team roster, and the Admin can pick one.
Submitting with a missing title, due date, or assignee shows inline validation and does not save (BR5).
Submitting a valid form saves the task and it appears in the All tasks list without a full page reload, with status Pending (R5, R8, BR4).
A signed-in User sees no Create/New Task entry point on /tasks, and visiting /tasks/new directly shows a permission-denied state (BR1, R9).
When the save request fails, the Admin sees an error message with a retry affordance and the form input is preserved (R12, NFR5).

manualTestChecklist: Sign in as an Admin, go to All tasks, click New Task -> you see a form with Title, Description, Due date, and an Assignee dropdown.
Open the Assignee dropdown -> you see the team members listed and can pick one.
Click Save with the Title empty -> you see a validation message and nothing is saved.
Fill in Title, pick a Due date and an Assignee, click Save -> the new task shows up in the All tasks list (status Pending) without the page reloading.
Sign in as a User and look at My tasks -> there is no New Task button anywhere.
As a User, type /tasks/new in the address bar -> you see a 'you do not have permission' message on the page (not an error page).

2.title: Delete a task
2.plainSummary: An Admin clicks Delete on a task in the All tasks list and is asked to confirm. Confirming removes the task from the list right away without reloading the page; cancelling keeps the task exactly as it was. A User never sees a Delete button.
2.summary: Add a Delete action to each row of the Admin All tasks list (TaskTable / TasksView). Clicking Delete opens a confirmation dialog; confirming calls deleteTask() (R7) and removes the row in place without a full reload; cancelling is a no-op. The Delete control is admin-only (BR1) — Users never render it. A failed delete shows an error with retry (R12, NFR5).

acceptanceCriteria.text: An Admin sees a Delete control on each task row in the All tasks list (BR1).
Clicking Delete opens a confirmation dialog naming the task before anything is removed.
Cancelling the dialog closes it and leaves the task in the list unchanged.
Confirming the dialog removes the task from the list without a full page reload (R7).
A signed-in User sees no Delete control on any task row (BR1).
When the delete request fails, the Admin sees an error message with a retry affordance and the task remains in the list (R12, NFR5).

manualTestChecklist: Sign in as an Admin and open All tasks -> each task row has a Delete control.
Click Delete on a task -> a confirmation dialog appears naming that task.
Click Cancel in the dialog -> the dialog closes and the task is still in the list.
Click Delete again and confirm -> the task disappears from the list without the page reloading.
Sign in as a User and open My tasks -> no row has a Delete control.
